title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a city cooking course.
audio_script: Speaker: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the information meeting for our new city cooking course. The course is for people who can already make a simple meal but want to learn some new ideas. It begins next Tuesday evening, and all classes are held in the kitchen at the community centre. Your main teacher will be Mrs [14]Bennett[/14], who has worked in restaurants in London and Manchester. She is very good at explaining things slowly, so beginners should not feel worried. The first class starts at [15]six forty-five[/15], although you can come ten minutes earlier to put on your apron and find your place. You do not need to bring pans or knives, because the centre has enough for everybody, but please bring a clean [16]towel[/16] each week because we do a lot of washing and drying. The first dish you will learn to make is a simple [17]vegetable soup[/17], which is cheap, healthy and easy to practise at home. Later in the course, you will make bread and a few desserts, but we start with easier food so that everyone feels confident. At the end of every class, everyone eats together in the [18]small hall[/18] beside the kitchen. This is a good chance to talk and ask questions. Please tell us before the first lesson if there is anything you cannot eat, and we will try to help. The full price for the course is forty pounds, but students and people over sixty-five pay [19]twenty-five[/19] pounds. If you want to join, please write your name on the list by the door before you leave today.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the main teacher will be Mrs Bennett.
The speaker says the first class starts at six forty-five.
The speaker says students should bring a clean towel each week.
The speaker says the first dish students will learn to make is vegetable soup.
The speaker says everyone eats together in the small hall beside the kitchen.
The speaker says students and people over sixty-five pay twenty-five pounds.
